tools.name: notarize_hash
tools.description: Anchor a sha256 digest in a Bitcoin SV transaction, proving the content existed at this time. Send ONLY the hash — the content never leaves your machine, and bsv.cx cannot see what was hashed. Free. Idempotent: the same hash returns the same record.

tools.name: verify_hash
tools.description: Look up a previously notarized sha256 and return when it was timestamped and the transaction that proves it. Free, and never rate-limited into unavailability — verifying a proof must always work.

tools.name: archive_url
tools.description: Capture a web page as it looks right now — screenshot and text — and anchor a hash of that capture on-chain. Use this before relying on a page you cite, so a later edit or deletion cannot be denied. Free.

tools.name: get_archive
tools.description: Fetch the manifest for a previous archive_url capture: when it was taken, and its proof. Free.

tools.name: verifiable_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a URL as readable text AND get a receipt anchoring a hash of the exact bytes returned, so you can later prove what the page said when you read it. 3 free calls, then priced per call.

tools.name: batch_timestamp
tools.description: Anchor a Merkle root over many sha256 digests in a single transaction — far cheaper per item than notarizing each one. Each hash gets an inclusion proof. 3 free calls, then priced per call.

tools.name: service_status
tools.description: Report whether anchoring is currently working and how much of the free trial remains. Call this if a tool reports something stored but not anchored.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "verify_hash" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Look up a previously notarized sha256 and return when it was timestamped and the transaction that proves it. Free, and never rate-limited into unavailability — …

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: verifiable_fetch · Private data: notarize_hash · Public sink: notarize_hash.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: verifiable_fetch · Private data: notarize_hash · Public sink: notarize_hash

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
